transcript: NT Live is all about trying to emulate a live performance in a cinema environment. It's a whole new experience for those who can't be in the theatre but feel they have a real connection to the live action on stage. Someone in Australia, in New Zealand, on a small little village in the UK will be able to see an amazing piece of theatre they otherwise won't have the chance to see it. Camera rehearsal one is the first time as an entire team that we're at the theatre. The morning of camera rehearsal one will start at 6.30 and we'll arrive and we will build the cameras. So that starts with building steel deck platforms within our auditorium, it might also include seat removals, we then rig the cameras onto these platforms. Once these are rigged we then go through a series of checks where we ensure that we've got communication lines between the auditorium and the OB truck. So the great thing about Inti Live is being able to go to lots of theatres around the West End and also regionally. So with Small Island we were in the Olivier Theatre, that's the theatre we've worked in a number of times before over the 10 years. Coming to do the Lehman Trilogy at the Piccadilly Theatre had its own set of challenges because it was the first time we'd been in this theatre. This rehearsal will run exactly as a normal performance would, in costume, full technical support. It's our first trial at seeing how we can broadcast the production. The job of the camera director is basically being in charge of the transfer from the theatre audience to the cinema audience. The way it works is that you go and watch it in the theatre and try and get a sense of where you want to put the cameras, whether you want the cameras to move or you want the cameras to be static and then you camera script so you basically get given a wide shot from from the back of the auditorium and you literally start going through the whole script writing in every camera shot we shot the first record yesterday today we all come and watch it projected in a cinema rather than just on a screen because we're shooting for cinema. We go round the room afterwards and everyone talks about the things that work and the things that don't work and if there's things that we can do better or everybody gets a chance to do that and then go away and everyone does their own thing to make the next one better if person sitting in the stalls in the Olivier theatre doesn't see the lace of the way I would like that person sitting at the cinema not to see the lens that's why I have to change it so we are absolutely sure that even on a tighter close-up we won't be able to see the lace so we won't spoil the magic of theater first camera rehearsals that is the time where we take all the notes and we start our planning those notes will have to be ready for second camera rehearsal and that is when we check if the notes that we apply are effectively worked or not. When you think about the whole NT Live production being this incredibly complex event there's so much going on my job is to come in early and rehearse the introduction just get a sense of how it all works and then when the actual moment comes they can rely on me just to welcome the audience in the theatre welcome the audience watching worldwide and the director and the whole production crew can concentrate on this amazing piece of theatre that's about to unfold. Before every show we go around to do what we call a shop check so we go and make sure everything's in the right position we check all the smaller details and that everything's good to go and it's just quite exciting to know that people are getting to see it all over the uk and worldwide as well i've got a friend who lives in sydney who was like i've saw one of your nc lives and i was like it's you know it's a really special thing that everyone gets to see today we are going to I'll be right back. I saw one of your NT lives and I was like it's you know it's a really special thing that everyone gets to see. Today we are going to be broadcasting the Lehman Trilogy. The broadcast starts just after seven o'clock so we at around about half past five have to make sure that our cinema can tune into the broadcast. You want the people who are sat in the audience to feel like they're in the theatre with the rest of the audience actually watching the show live in that venue. Hello and welcome to the Piccadilly Theatre in London's West End for this live broadcast of the Lehman Trilogy directed for the National Theatre by Sam Lenders. It's a very exciting experience doing NT Live. Essentially, you just do the performance that you would normally do. And of course, that's the point about NT Live. That's the big plus about the whole experiment. As an audience in cinemas, you will see what you will see on stage if you were to go to the theatre. The only difference is there's a slight buzz of excitement about the fact that it's being broadcast all over the place. So you get these messages at the interval going loving it in Finland or you know whatever. Now that's thrilling, that's thrilling.
